p_exactOut_outputConservation","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"pure","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"uint160","name":"sqrtPriceCurrent","type":"uint160"},{"internalType":"uint160","name":"sqrtPriceTarget","type":"uint160"},{"internalType":"uint128","name":"liquidity","type":"uint128"},{"internalType":"int256","name":"amountRemaining","type":"int256"},{"internalType":"uint24","name":"feePips","type":"uint24"}],"name":"testFuzz_computeSwapStep_feeNonNegative","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"pure","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"uint160","name":"sqrtPriceCurrent","type":"uint160"},{"internalType":"uint160","name":"sqrtPriceTarget","type":"uint160"},{"internalType":"uint128","name":"liquidity","type":"uint128"},{"internalType":"int256","name":"amountRemaining","type":"int256"},{"internalType":"uint24","name":"feePips","type":"uint24"}],"name":"testFuzz_computeSwapStep_priceBounds","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"pure","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"uint160","name":"sqrtPriceCurrentX96","type":"uint160"},{"internalType":"uint160","name":"sqrtPriceNextTickX96","type":"uint160"},{"internalType":"uint160","name":"sqrtPriceLimitX96","type":"uint160"},{"internalType":"uint128","name":"liquidity","type":"uint128"},{"internalType":"int256","name":"amountRemaining","type":"int256"},{"internalType":"uint24","name":"feePips","type":"uint24"}],"name":"testFuzz_computeSwapStep_sqrtPriceLimitNeverCrossed","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"pure","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"bool","name":"zeroForOne","type":"bool"},{"internalType":"uint160","name":"sqrtPriceNextX96","type":"uint160"},{"internalType":"uint160","name":"sqrtPriceLimitX96","type":"uint160"}],"name":"testFuzz_getSqrtPriceTarget_correctSelection","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"pure","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"uint160","name":"sqrtPriceCurrent","type":"uint160"},{"internalType":"uint160","name":"sqrtPriceTarget","type":"uint160"},{"internalType":"uint128","name":"liquidity","type":"uint128"},{"internalType":"uint24","name":"feePips","type":"uint24"}],"name":"test_computeSwapStep_int256Min_edgeCase","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"pure","type":"function"}],"devdoc":{"details":"Tests invariants of computeSwapStep and getSqrtPriceTarget. Key invariants tested: 1. For exact input swaps: amountIn + feeAmount <= abs(amountRemaining) 2. For exact output swaps: amountOut <= abs(amountRemaining) 3. sqrtPriceNext is bounded between current price and target price 4. Fee calculations are always non-negative 5. getSqrtPriceTarget returns correct min/max based on direction","kind":"dev","methods":{"testFuzz_computeSwapStep_allInvariants(uint160,uint160,uint128,int256,uint24)":{"details":"Tests all acceptance criteria in a single fuzz test for efficiency.      NOTE: This test intentionally allows amountRemaining == type(int256).min.      When negating int256.min in unchecked Solidity, uint256(-int256.min) == 2^255,      which is mathematically correct in two's complement arithmetic. This edge case      is explicitly tested in test_computeSwapStep_int256Min_edgeCase.","params":{"amountRemaining":"Remaining amount to swap (negative for exact in, positive for exact out)","feePips":"Fee in hundredths of a bip","liquidity":"Pool liquidity","sqrtPriceRaw":"Current sqrt price (raw input, will be bounded)","sqrtPriceTargetRaw":"Target sqrt price (raw input, will be bounded)"}},"testFuzz_computeSwapStep_directionConsistency(uint160,uint160,uint128,uint256,uint24)":{"details":"zeroForOne means price decreases; oneForZero means price increases","params":{"amount":"Amount for the swap","feePips":"Fee in hundredths of a bip","liquidity":"Pool liquidity","sqrtPriceCurrent":"Current sqrt price","sqrtPriceTarget":"Target sqrt price"}},"testFuzz_computeSwapStep_exactIn_inputConservation(uint160,uint160,uint128,uint256,uint24)":{"details":"Specifically tests the exact input invariant with focused input ranges","params":{"feePips":"Fee in hundredths of a bip","inputAmount":"Amount of input tokens (will be negated for amountRemaining)","liquidity":"Pool liquidity","sqrtPriceCurrent":"Current sqrt price","sqrtPriceTarget":"Target sqrt price"}},"testFuzz_computeSwapStep_exactOut_outputConservation(uint160,uint160,uint128,uint256,uint24)":{"details":"Specifically tests the exact output invariant with focused input ranges","params":{"feePips":"Fee in hundredths of a bip (must be < 100% for exact out)","liquidity":"Pool liquidity","outputAmount":"Amount of output tokens requested","sqrtPriceCurrent":"Current sqrt price","sqrtPriceTarget":"Target sqrt price"}},"testFuzz_computeSwapStep_feeNonNegative(uint160,uint160,uint128,int256,uint24)":{"details":"Verifies feeAmount is never negative (uint256 guarantees this, but we verify no underflow)","params":{"amountRemaining":"Amount remaining","feePips":"Fee in hundredths of a bip","liquidity":"Pool liquidity","sqrtPriceCurrent":"Current sqrt price","sqrtPriceTarget":"Target sqrt price"}},"testFuzz_computeSwapStep_priceBounds(uint160,uint160,uint128,int256,uint24)":{"details":"Tests that the resulting price never exceeds the boundaries","params":{"amountRemaining":"Amount remaining (negative = exact in, positive = exact out)","feePips":"Fee in hundredths of a bip","liquidity":"Pool liquidity","sqrtPriceCurrent":"Current sqrt price","sqrtPriceTarget":"Target sqrt price"}},"testFuzz_computeSwapStep_sqrtPriceLimitNeverCrossed(uint160,uint160,uint160,uint128,int256,uint24)":{"details":"This test validates the swap loop composition by:      1. Generating (sqrtPriceCurrentX96, sqrtPriceNextTickX96, sqrtPriceLimitX96)      2. Deriving sqrtPriceTargetX96 via getSqrtPriceTarget      3. Asserting sqrtPriceNextX96 never crosses sqrtPriceLimitX96      The existing tests bound sqrtPriceNextX96 vs the *target*, which is correct      for computeSwapStep in isolation. This test validates the intended call      composition used by pool swap loops where the limit is the user-specified      price boundary.","params":{"amountRemaining":"Amount remaining (negative = exact in, positive = exact out)","feePips":"Fee in hundredths of a bip","liquidity":"Pool liquidity","sqrtPriceCurrentX96":"Current sqrt price (will be bounded)","sqrtPriceLimitX96":"User-specified sqrt price limit (will be bounded)","sqrtPriceNextTickX96":"Sqrt price at the next initialized tick (will be bounded)"}},"testFuzz_getSqrtPriceTarget_correctSelection(bool,uint160,uint160)":{"details":"When zeroForOne, returns max(next, limit); when oneForZero, returns min(next, limit)","params":{"sqrtPriceLimitX96":"The sqrt price limit for the swap","sqrtPriceNextX96":"The sqrt price for the next initialized tick","zeroForOne":"Direction of the swap"}},"test_computeSwapStep_int256Min_edgeCase(uint160,uint160,uint128,uint24)":{"details":"When amountRemaining == type(int256).min (-2^255), the negation cannot be      represented as int256 (since int256.max is 2^255 - 1). However, in unchecked      Solidity, uint256(-int256.min) correctly evaluates to 2^255 due to two's      complement arithmetic.      This test explicitly documents that:      1. int256.min is a valid input to computeSwapStep      2. The unchecked negation uint256(-int256.min) == 2^255 is intentional      3. All invariants hold for this extreme input value      We chose to ALLOW int256.min rather than exclude it via vm.assume because:      - The SwapMath library handles it correctly      - Excluding it would reduce test coverage      - The behavior is mathematically well-defined in two's complement","params":{"feePips":"Fee in hundredths of a bip (exact input allows up to 100%)","liquidity":"Pool liquidity","sqrtPriceCurrent":"Current sqrt price (bounded to valid range)","sqrtPriceTarget":"Target sqrt price (bounded to valid range)"}}},"stateVariables":{"MAX_SQRT_PRICE":{"details":"Maximum sqrt price from TickMath"},"MAX_SWAP_FEE":{"details":"Maximum swap fee is 100% (1e6 hundredths of a bip)"},"MIN_SQRT_PRICE":{"details":"Minimum sqrt price from TickMath"}},"title":"SwapMath_V4_Fuzz_Test","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"testFuzz_computeSwapStep_allInvariants(uint160,uint160,uint128,int256,uint24)":{"notice":"Comprehensive fuzz test for computeSwapStep covering all invariants"},"testFuzz_computeSwapStep_directionConsistency(uint160,uint160,uint128,uint256,uint24)":{"notice":"Fuzz test: Price movement direction is consistent with swap direction"},"testFuzz_computeSwapStep_exactIn_inputConservation(uint160,uint160,uint128,uint256,uint24)":{"notice":"Fuzz test: exactIn invariant - amountIn + feeAmount <= abs(amountRemaining)"},"testFuzz_computeSwapStep_exactOut_outputConservation(uint160,uint160,uint128,uint256,uint24)":{"notice":"Fuzz test: exactOut invariant - amountOut <= amountRemaining"},"testFuzz_computeSwapStep_feeNonNegative(uint160,uint160,uint128,int256,uint24)":{"notice":"Fuzz test: Fee calculations are always non-negative"},"testFuzz_computeSwapStep_priceBounds(uint160,uint160,uint128,int256,uint24)":{"notice":"Fuzz test: sqrtPriceNext is bounded by current and target prices"},"testFuzz_computeSwapStep_sqrtPriceLimitNeverCrossed(uint160,uint160,uint160,uint128,int256,uint24)":{"notice":"Fuzz test: sqrtPriceNextX96 never crosses sqrtPriceLimitX96"},"testFuzz_getSqrtPriceTarget_correctSelection(bool,uint160,uint160)":{"notice":"Fuzz test: getSqrtPriceTarget returns the correct target based on direction"},"test_computeSwapStep_int256Min_edgeCase(uint160,uint160,uint128,uint24)":{"notice":"Explicit test documenting the int256.min edge case behavior"}},"notice":"Fuzz tests for Uniswap V4 SwapMath library to discover edge cases via randomized testFuzz_computeSwapStep_allInvariants(u.details: Tests all acceptance criteria in a single fuzz test for efficiency.      NOTE: This test intentionally allows amountRemaining == type(int256).min.      When negating int256.min in unchecked Solidity, uint256(-int256.min) == 2^255,      which is mathematically correct in two's complement arithmetic. This edge case      is explicitly tested in test_computeSwapStep_int256Min_edgeCase.

params.amountRemaining: Remaining amount to swap (negative for exact in, positive for exact out)
params.feePips: Fee in hundredths of a bip
params.sqrtPriceRaw: Current sqrt price (raw input, will be bounded)
params.sqrtPriceTargetRaw: Target sqrt price (raw input, will be bounded)

testFuzz_computeSwapStep_directionConsis.details: zeroForOne means price decreases; oneForZero means price increases

params.feePips: Fee in hundredths of a bip

testFuzz_computeSwapStep_exactIn_inputCo.details: Specifically tests the exact input invariant with focused input ranges

params.feePips: Fee in hundredths of a bip
params.inputAmount: Amount of input tokens (will be negated for amountRemaining)

testFuzz_computeSwapStep_exactOut_output.details: Specifically tests the exact output invariant with focused input ranges

params.feePips: Fee in hundredths of a bip (must be < 100% for exact out)

testFuzz_computeSwapStep_feeNonNegative(.details: Verifies feeAmount is never negative (uint256 guarantees this, but we verify no underflow)

params.feePips: Fee in hundredths of a bip

testFuzz_computeSwapStep_priceBounds(uin.details: Tests that the resulting price never exceeds the boundaries

params.amountRemaining: Amount remaining (negative = exact in, positive = exact out)
params.feePips: Fee in hundredths of a bip

testFuzz_computeSwapStep_sqrtPriceLimitN.details: This test validates the swap loop composition by:      1. Generating (sqrtPriceCurrentX96, sqrtPriceNextTickX96, sqrtPriceLimitX96)      2. Deriving sqrtPriceTargetX96 via getSqrtPriceTarget      3. Asserting sqrtPriceNextX96 never crosses sqrtPriceLimitX96      The existing tests bound sqrtPriceNextX96 vs the *target*, which is correct      for computeSwapStep in isolation. This test validates the intended call      composition used by pool swap loops where the limit is the user-specified      price boundary.

params.amountRemaining: Amount remaining (negative = exact in, positive = exact out)
params.feePips: Fee in hundredths of a bip
params.sqrtPriceLimitX96: User-specified sqrt price limit (will be bounded)
params.sqrtPriceNextTickX96: Sqrt price at the next initialized tick (will be bounded)

testFuzz_getSqrtPriceTarget_correctSelec.details: When zeroForOne, returns max(next, limit); when oneForZero, returns min(next, limit)

params.sqrtPriceLimitX96: The sqrt price limit for the swap
params.sqrtPriceNextX96: The sqrt price for the next initialized tick

test_computeSwapStep_int256Min_edgeCase(.details: When amountRemaining == type(int256).min (-2^255), the negation cannot be      represented as int256 (since int256.max is 2^255 - 1). However, in unchecked      Solidity, uint256(-int256.min) correctly evaluates to 2^255 due to two's      complement arithmetic.      This test explicitly documents that:      1. int256.min is a valid input to computeSwapStep      2. The unchecked negation uint256(-int256.min) == 2^255 is intentional      3. All invariants hold for this extreme input value      We chose to ALLOW int256.min rather than exclude it via vm.assume because:      - The SwapMath library handles it correctly      - Excluding it would reduce test coverage      - The behavior is mathematically well-defined in two's complement

params.feePips: Fee in hundredths of a bip (exact input allows up to 100%)
params.sqrtPriceCurrent: Current sqrt price (bounded to valid range)
params.sqrtPriceTarget: Target sqrt price (bounded to valid range)

testFuzz_computeSwapStep_allInvariants(u.notice: Comprehensive fuzz test for computeSwapStep covering all invariants

testFuzz_computeSwapStep_directionConsis.notice: Fuzz test: Price movement direction is consistent with swap direction

testFuzz_computeSwapStep_exactIn_inputCo.notice: Fuzz test: exactIn invariant - amountIn + feeAmount <= abs(amountRemaining)

testFuzz_computeSwapStep_exactOut_output.notice: Fuzz test: exactOut invariant - amountOut <= amountRemaining

testFuzz_computeSwapStep_feeNonNegative(.notice: Fuzz test: Fee calculations are always non-negative

testFuzz_computeSwapStep_priceBounds(uin.notice: Fuzz test: sqrtPriceNext is bounded by current and target prices

testFuzz_computeSwapStep_sqrtPriceLimitN.notice: Fuzz test: sqrtPriceNextX96 never crosses sqrtPriceLimitX96

testFuzz_getSqrtPriceTarget_correctSelec.notice: Fuzz test: getSqrtPriceTarget returns the correct target based on direction

test_computeSwapStep_int256Min_edgeCase(.notice: Explicit test documenting the int256.min edge case behavior
